However, the growing scale of these optimization problems have outpaced commercial solvers commonly employed in production. To accelerate resource allocation, prior approaches either customize solutions for narrow domains or impose workload-specific assumptions. In this work, we revisit real-world resource allocation problems and uncover a common underlying structure: the vast majority of these problems are inherently separable, i.e., they optimize the aggregate utility of individual resource and demand allocations, under separate constraints for each resource and each demand. Building on this observation, we develop DeDe, a scalable and theoretically rooted optimization framework for large-scale resource allocation. At the core of DeDe is a decouple-and-decompose approach: it decouples entangled resource and demand constraints and thereby decomposes the overall optimization into alternating per-resource and per-demand subproblems that can be solved efficiently and in parallel. We have implemented and released DeDe as a Python package with a familiar modeling interface. Our experiments on three representative resource allocation tasks -- cluster scheduling, traffic engineering, and load balancing -- demonstrate that DeDe delivers significant speedups while generating higher-quality allocations.
items.abstract_zh: 在云系统中，高效的资源分配对于促进租户之间的资源共享至关重要。然而，这些优化问题的规模不断扩大，已经超过了生产中常用的商业求解器。为了加速资源分配，先前的方法要么为狭窄的领域定制解决方案，要么强加特定于工作负载的假设。在这项工作中，我们重新审视现实世界的资源分配问题，并揭示了一个共同的底层结构：这些问题中的绝大多数本质上是可分离的，即它们在每种资源和每种需求的单独约束下优化单个资源和需求分配的总效用。基于这一观察，我们开发了 DeDe，一个可扩展且有理论基础的优化框架，用于大规模资源分配。 DeDe 的核心是一种解耦和分解方法：它解耦了纠缠的资源和需求约束，从而将整体优化分解为交替的每个资源和每个需求的子问题，这些子问题可以有效地并行解决。我们已经实现并发布了 DeDe 作为 Python 包，具有​​熟悉的建模界面。我们对三个代表性资源分配任务（集群调度、流量工程和负载平衡）进行的实验表明，DeDe 在生成更高质量的分配的同时提供了显着的加速。

items.title: Deterministic Client: Enforcing Determinism on Untrusted Machine Code.
items.abstract: This paper presents Deterministic Client (DeCl), a software-based sandboxing system for enforcing deterministic behavior on untrusted machine code, either x86-64 or Arm64. DeCl adapts techniques from Software Fault Isolation (SFI) traditionally used to guarantee memory isolation to instead enforce the stronger property of determinism. By using a simple and efficient machine code verifier that can guarantee that a program behaves deterministically, DeCl does not rely on a trusted compiler/interpreter for correctness. This allows the use of LLVM without compromising the size of the trusted code base. We also describe how to implement two efficient metering mechanisms that enforce deterministic preemption of sandboxed programs, and how DeCl can be implemented in combination with traditional software-based isolation, by making the sandboxed code position-oblivious. DeCl is able to combine and improve upon the benefits of both interpreters and JIT compilers at once, with low CPU overhead, fast startup time, and strong security via a small trusted code base. We evaluate DeCl's effectiveness on general-purpose CPU benchmarks, as well as in an application-specific context by integrating with the Groundhog smart contract engine, and using DeCl for zero-knowledge-proof verification.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了确定性客户端 (DeCl)，这是一种基于软件的沙箱系统，用于在不受信任的机器代码（x86-64 或 Arm64）上实施确定性行为。 DeCl 采用了传统上用于保证内存隔离的软件故障隔离 (SFI) 技术，以强制执行更强的确定性属性。通过使用可以保证程序确定性运行的简单高效的机器代码验证器，DeCl 不依赖于受信任的编译器/解释器来确保正确性。这允许在不影响可信代码库大小的情况下使用 LLVM。我们还描述了如何实现两种有效的计量机制来强制沙盒程序的确定性抢占，以及如何通过使沙盒代码位置无关来与传统的基于软件的隔离相结合来实现 DeCl。 DeCl 能够同时结合和改进解释器和 JIT 编译器的优点，具有低 CPU 开销、快速启动时间以及通过小型可信代码库实现的强大安全性。我们通过与 Groundhog 智能合约引擎集成并使用 DeCl 进行零知识证明验证，评估了 DeCl 在通用 CPU 基准测试以及特定应用程序环境中的有效性。

items.title: KRR: Efficient and Scalable Kernel Record Replay.
items.abstract:
Modern kernels are large, complex, and plagued with bugs. Unfortunately, their large size and complexity make kernel failures very challenging for developers to diagnose since failures encountered in deployment are often notoriously difficult to reproduce. Although record-replay techniques provide the powerful ability to accurately record a failed execution and deterministically replay it, enabling advanced manual and automated analysis techniques, they are inefficient and do not scale with modern I/O-intensive, concurrent workloads.

This paper introduces KRR, a kernel record-replay framework that provides a highly efficient execution recording mechanism by narrowing the scope of the record and replay boundary to the kernel. Unlike previous record-replay whole-stack approaches, KRR adopts a split-recorder design that employs the guest and the host to jointly record the kernel execution. Our evaluation demonstrates that KRR scales efficiently up to 8 cores, across a range of different workloads, including kernel compilation, RocksDB, and Nginx. When recording 8-core VMs that run RocksDB and kernel compilation, KRR incurs only a 1.52× ~ 2.79× slowdown compared to native execution, while traditional whole-VM RR suffers from 8.97× ~ 29.94× slowdown. We validate that KRR is practical and has a broad recording scope by reproducing 17 bugs across different Linux versions, including 6 non-deterministic bugs and 5 high-risk CVEs; KRR was able to record and reproduce all but one non-deterministic bug.
items.abstract_zh:
现代内核庞大、复杂并且充满错误。不幸的是，它们的大尺寸和复杂性使得开发人员诊断内核故障非常困难，因为部署中遇到的故障通常很难重现。尽管记录重放技术提供了准确记录失败的执行并确定性地重放它的强大能力，从而支持高级手动和自动分析技术，但它们效率低下，并且无法随着现代 I/O 密集型并发工作负载进行扩展。

本文介绍了KRR，一个内核记录重放框架，它通过将记录和重放边界范围缩小到内核来提供高效的执行记录机制。与之前的记录-重放全栈方法不同，KRR 采用分割记录器设计，利用 guest 和 host 共同记录内核执行。我们的评估表明，KRR 可有效扩展到 8 个内核，适用于一系列不同的工作负载，包括内核编译、RocksDB 和 Nginx。当记录运行 RocksDB 和内核编译的 8 核虚拟机时，与本机执行相比，KRR 仅导致 1.52× ~ 2.79× 的减速，而传统的整体 VM RR 则遭受 8.97× ~ 29.94× 的减速。我们通过在不同 Linux 版本上重现 17 个 bug（包括 6 个非确定性 bug 和 5 个高风险 CVE）来验证 KRR 的实用性和记录范围广泛； KRR 能够记录和重现除一个非确定性错误之外的所有错误。

items.title: Basilisk: Using Provenance Invariants to Automate Proofs of Undecidable Protocols.
items.abstract:
Distributed protocols are challenging to design correctly. One promising approach to improve their reliability uses formal verification to prove that a protocol satisfies a desired safety property. These proofs require finding an inductive invariant that holds initially, implies safety, and is inductive. Devising an inductive invariant is a difficult task that prior work has either automated by requiring the protocol to be expressed in a decidable but restrictive fragment of logic, or required the developer to find by a painful search process.

In this work we aim to automatically find inductive invariants without restricting the logic. We do so using two key insights. Our first insight is that many of the complex inter-host properties that prior work required the developer to provide can instead be derived using Provenance Invariants , a class of invariants that relate a local variable in a host to its provenance, i.e., the protocol step that caused it to have its current value. By tracing the provenance of one host variable back to another host, we can derive an invariant relating the two hosts' states. Second, we develop an algorithm called Atomic Sharding to derive Provenance Invariants automatically by statically analyzing the protocol's steps.

We implement these ideas in a tool called Basilisk and apply it to 16 distributed protocols. Basilisk automatically finds a set of invariants and proves their inductiveness, with little or no developer assistance. In all cases, these generated invariants are sufficient for us to prove safety without needing to identify any new inductive invariants.
items.abstract_zh:
正确设计分布式协议具有挑战性。提高其可靠性的一种有前途的方法是使用形式验证来证明协议满足所需的安全属性。这些证明需要找到一个归纳不变量，该不变量最初成立，意味着安全，并且是归纳的。设计归纳不变量是一项艰巨的任务，之前的工作要么通过要求协议以可判定但限制性的逻辑片段来表达来实现自动化，要么需要开发人员通过痛苦的搜索过程来找到。

在这项工作中，我们的目标是在不限制逻辑的情况下自动找到归纳不变量。我们使用两个关键见解来做到这一点。我们的第一个见解是，以前的工作需要开发人员提供的许多复杂的主机间属性可以使用来源不变量派生，这是一类将主机中的局部变量与其来源相关联的不变量，即导致其具有当前值的协议步骤。通过将一个主机变量的来源追溯到另一​​台主机，我们可以导出与两个主机状态相关的不变量。其次，我们开发了一种称为“原子分片”的算法，通过静态分析协议的步骤来自动导出来源不变量。

我们在名为 Basilisk 的工具中实现了这些想法，并将其应用于 16 个分布式协议。 Basilisk 会自动找到一组不变量并证明它们的归纳性，几乎不需要开发人员的帮助。在所有情况下，这些生成的不变量足以让我们证明安全性，而不需要识别任何新的归纳不变量。

items.title: BlitzScale: Fast and Live Large Model Autoscaling with O(1) Host Caching.
items.abstract: Model autoscaling is the key mechanism to achieve serverless model-as-a-service, but it faces a fundamental trade-off between scaling speed and storage/memory usage to cache parameters, and cannot meet frequent scaling requirements across multiple hosts. The key problem is that data plane performance is slow, and scaled instances remain stopped while parameters are loading. In this paper, we first show that the data plane can be made fast with no or O(1) caching by loading parameters through the compute network between GPUs because: (1) its speed is comparable to host cache and is underutilized, and (2) scaling multiple instances requires no or O(1) caching with network-optimized multicast. Second, autoscaling can be made live by breaking the scaling abstraction for inference from a coarse-grained instance-level to a fine-grained layer-level. This allows us to offload the layer computation from the overloaded serving instances to the scaled ones without waiting for the parameters to be fully loaded. Under real-world workloads, our system BLITZSCALE achieves up to 94 % lower tail latency reductions compared to state-of-the-art autoscaling system (ServerlessLLM), and it reduces the GPU time used for serving by 49 % when compared with serving systems that do not support autoscaling like DistServe and vLLM with the same service-level-agreement.
items.abstract_zh: 模型自动伸缩是实现无服务器模型即服务的关键机制，但它面临着伸缩速度和缓存参数的存储/内存使用之间的基本权衡，并且无法满足跨多个主机的频繁伸缩需求。关键问题是数据平面性能缓慢，并且在加载参数时缩放实例保持停止状态。在本文中，我们首先证明，通过通过 GPU 之间的计算网络加载参数，可以在没有缓存或 O(1) 缓存的情况下加快数据平面的速度，因为：(1) 其速度与主机缓存相当，并且未得到充分利用，(2) 扩展多个实例不需要使用网络优化的多播或 O(1) 缓存。其次，可以通过打破从粗粒度实例级别到细粒度层级别的推理的缩放抽象来实现自动缩放。这允许我们将层计算从超载的服务实例卸载到缩放的服务实例，而无需等待参数完全加载。在实际工作负载下，与最先进的自动扩展系统 (ServerlessLLM) 相比，我们的系统 BLITZSCALE 的尾部延迟降低了 94%，并且与具有相同服务级别协议的 DistServe 和 vLLM 等不支持自动扩展的服务系统相比，用于服务的 GPU 时间减少了 49%。

items.title: Extending Applications Safely and Efficiently.
items.abstract: This paper presents the Extension Interface Model (EIM) and bpftime, which together enable safer and more efficient extension of userspace applications than the current state-of-the-art. EIM is a new model that treats each required feature of an extension as a resource, including concrete hardware resources (e.g., memory) and abstract ones (e.g., the ability to invoke a function from the extended application). An extension manager, i.e., the person who manages a deployment, uses EIM to specify only the resources an extension needs to perform its task. bpftime is a new extension framework that enforces an EIM specification. Compared to prior systems, bpftime is efficient because it uses extended Berkeley Packet Filter (eBPF)-style verification, hardware-supported isolation features (e.g., Intel MPK), and dynamic binary rewriting. Moreover, bpftime is easy to adopt into existing workflows since it is compatible with the current eBPF ecosystem. We demonstrate the usefulness of EIM and bpftime across 6 use cases that improve security, monitor and enhance performance, and explore configuration trade-offs.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了扩展接口模型 (EIM) 和 bpftime，它们共同实现了比当前最先进技术更安全、更高效的用户空间应用程序扩展。 EIM 是一种新模型，它将扩展所需的每个功能视为资源，包括具体的硬件资源（例如内存）和抽象的硬件资源（例如从扩展应用程序调用功能的能力）。扩展管理员（即管理部署的人员）使用 EIM 来仅指定扩展执行其任务所需的资源。 bpftime 是一个强制执行 EIM 规范的新扩展框架。与之前的系统相比，bpftime 非常高效，因为它使用扩展的 Berkeley Packet Filter (eBPF) 式验证、硬件支持的隔离功能（例如 Intel MPK）和动态二进制重写。此外，bpftime 很容易采用现有工作流程，因为它与当前的 eBPF 生态系统兼容。我们通过 6 个用例展示了 EIM 和 bpftime 的实用性，这些用例可提高安全性、监控和增强性能以及探索配置权衡。

items.title: NanoFlow: Towards Optimal Large Language Model Serving Throughput.
items.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have resulted in a surging demand for planet-scale serving systems, where tens of thousands of GPUs continuously serve hundreds of millions of users. Consequently, throughput has emerged as a key metric that determines serving systems' performance. Due to large model sizes and memory-intensive self-attention, LLM serving has been commonly assumed to be memory-bound. Through a detailed analysis, we show that despite having memory-intensive components, end-to-end LLM serving is compute bound for most common workloads and LLMs. Alas, most existing serving engines fall short from optimal compute utilization, because the heterogeneous operations that comprise LLM serving--compute, memory, networking--are executed sequentially within a device. We propose NanoFlow, a novel serving framework that exploits intra-device parallelism, which overlaps the usage of heterogeneous resources within a single device. NanoFlow splits inputs into smaller nano-batches and duplicates operations to operate on each portion independently, enabling overlapping. NanoFlow automatically identifies the number, size, ordering, and GPU resource allocation of nano-batches to minimize the execution time, while considering the interference of concurrent operations. We evaluate NanoFlow's end-to-end serving throughput on several popular models such as LLaMA-2-70B, Mixtral 8x7B, LLaMA-3-8B, etc. With practical workloads, NanoFlow provides 1.91x throughput boost compared to state-of-the-art serving systems achieving 50% to 72% of optimal throughput across popular models.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 导致了对全球服务系统的需求激增，其中数以万计的 GPU 持续为数亿用户提供服务。因此，吞吐量已成为决定服务系统性能的关键指标。由于模型尺寸较大和内存密集型自注意力机制，LLM 服务通常被认为是受内存限制的。通过详细分析，我们表明，尽管具有内存密集型组件，但端到端 LLM 服务对于大多数常见工作负载和 LLM 来说都是计算限制的。遗憾的是，大多数现有服务引擎都无法实现最佳计算利用率，因为构成 LLM 服务的异构操作（计算、内存、网络）是在设备内按顺序执行的。我们提出了 NanoFlow，一种利用设备内并行性的新颖服务框架，它与单个设备内异构资源的使用重叠。 NanoFlow 将输入分割成更小的纳米批次，并重复操作以独立地对每个部分进行操作，从而实现重叠。 NanoFlow自动识别纳米批次的数量、大小、顺序和GPU资源分配，以最大限度地减少执行时间，同时考虑并发操作的干扰。我们在 LLaMA-2-70B、Mixtral 8x7B、LLaMA-3-8B 等多种流行模型上评估了 NanoFlow 的端到端服务吞吐量。在实际工作负载下，NanoFlow 与最先进的服务系统相比，吞吐量提高了 1.91 倍，在流行模型中实现了最佳吞吐量的 50% 至 72%。

items.title: Compass: Encrypted Semantic Search with High Accuracy.
items.abstract: We present Compass, a semantic search system for encrypted data that achieves high accuracy, matching state-of-the-art plaintext search quality, while ensuring the privacy of data, queries, and results, even if the server is compromised. Compass contributes a novel way to traverse a state-of-the-art graph-based semantic search index and a white-box co-design with Oblivious RAM, a cryptographic primitive that hides access patterns, to enable efficient search over encrypted embeddings. With our techniques, Directional Neighbor Filtering, Speculative Neighbor Prefetch, and Graph-Traversal Tailored ORAM, Compass achieves user-perceived latencies within or around a second and is orders of magnitude faster than baselines under various network conditions.
items.abstract_zh: 我们推出了 Compass，这是一种用于加密数据的语义搜索系统，即使服务器受到威胁，也能实现高精度、与最先进的明文搜索质量相匹配，同时确保数据、查询和结果的隐私。 Compass 提供了一种新颖的方式来遍历最先进的基于图形的语义搜索索引，并与 Oblivious RAM（一种隐藏访问模式的加密原语）进行白盒协同设计，以实现对加密嵌入的高效搜索。借助我们的定向邻居过滤、推测邻居预取和图遍历定制 ORAM 等技术，Compass 可以在一秒或一秒左右实现用户感知的延迟，并且在各种网络条件下比基线快几个数量级。
